gpt_review.biasedText: China has a chokehold on the minerals which are used in the making of electric cars, electronics and military weapons.
gpt_review.reason: This aligns with my detection of 'chokehold' as loaded, dramatic word choice. GPT categorizes it differently but identifies the same core issue.

gpt_review.biasedText: These curbs have dealt a major blow to the US, whose industries are heavily dependent on imports of the precious metal.
gpt_review.reason: This matches my detection of the same text. The framing as opinion presented as fact is valid, though I categorized it as spin.

gpt_review.biasedText: It is the reason why Beijing's export curbs have the ability to hit the US hard.
gpt_review.reason: I didn't flag this specific sentence, but 'hit the US hard' is indeed dramatic language that amplifies impact beyond neutral description. It's a reasonable detection.

gpt_review.biasedText: China has a near monopoly on extracting rare earths
gpt_review.reason: The article itself provides data showing China accounts for 61% of production and 92% of processing. 'Near monopoly' is actually a reasonable characterization supported by the data presented, especially regarding processing. This is not biased language.

gpt_review.biasedText: This is largely believed to be part of why US president Donald Trump is so keen to sign a minerals deal with Ukraine - it wants to reduce dependency on China.
gpt_review.reason: 'So keen' does inject an emotional characterization of Trump's motivation. This is a defensible detection of editorializing language.

gpt_review.biasedText: Another place Trump has had his eye on is Greenland which is endowed with the eighth largest reserves of rare earth elements.
gpt_review.reason: 'Had his eye on' is indeed colloquial and interpretive, though it's a relatively mild instance. Still defensible as informal, non-neutral framing.

gpt_review.biasedText: the adversarial tone Trump has struck with them means the US could be left with very few alternative suppliers.
gpt_review.reason: This matches my detection of the same text. The author's characterization of Trump's tone as 'adversarial' and the causal claim are presented as authorial fact rather than attributed analysis.

sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet returned zero detections, missing this one-sided framing that attributes supply problems solely to Trump's diplomacy.

sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet returned zero detections, missing the inaccurate and loaded use of 'precious metal' to describe rare earths.

gpt_false_negatives.reason: GPT missed this loaded metaphor implying menacing, coercive control. A neutral phrasing would be 'increased restrictions on.'
